svg.title: Two approaches to the context window problem: long context vs external memory
svg.desc: Side by side comparison of two solutions. Left side shows a long-context model fitting an entire book, codebase, or conversation into one giant window. Right side shows an external memory system where facts are written to and retrieved from a vector store on demand.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How large can a long-context model window get?')
sendPrompt('Can a long-context model read a whole PDF?')
sendPrompt('What is the largest context window available today?')
sendPrompt('What is the largest context window available today?')
sendPrompt('What LLMs support 1 million token context windows?')
sendPrompt('How does an LLM write to external memory?')
sendPrompt('What is a vector store used for in LLM memory?')
sendPrompt('How does semantic retrieval from memory work?')
sendPrompt('How does external memory make LLMs more efficient?')

svg.text: rest lives in the memory store
